scope_note: Harvard does not publish GPA data by major. The figures below are official school-wide first-year GPA statistics from Harvard's Common Data Set plus Harvard's published holistic-review language.
policy_summary: Harvard does not publish a minimum GPA cutoff on its application requirements page. In Harvard's 2023-24 Common Data Set, the average high school GPA for first-time, first-year students who submitted GPA was 4.2, and 74.02% of enrolled students were reported in the 4.0 GPA bin on the CDS 4.0-scale ranges.

what_school_does_say.claim: Harvard does not list a minimum required GPA for first-year applicants on its application requirements page.

Harvard says high school grades in a rigorous academic program can help assess readiness, but most applicants and admitted students already have outstanding academic records, so grades alone are hard to use to differentiate applications.
Harvard's 2023-24 Common Data Set reports an average high school GPA of 4.2 for first-time, first-year students who submitted GPA.

The same Common Data Set reports that 74.02% of enrolled first-year students were in the 4.0 GPA bin, 19.67% were between 3.75 and 3.99, and 4.10% were between 3.50 and 3.74 on its 4.0-scale GPA ranges.
